headlines.title: Exclusive: Trump says Iran hasn't made a deal because ‘they’re strong’ and ‘proud’
headlines.description: President Trump sits down for an exclusive interview with Meet the Press as the U.S. looks to make a deal with Iran to end the war.

headlines.title: Democrat Xavier Becerra advances to general election in California governor’s race
headlines.description: Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra has advanced to the general election in the California governor’s race, NBC News projects

headlines.title: Putin rejects Zelenskyy's invitation to meet face-to-face
headlines.description: Russia's President Vladimir Putin rejected an invitation to meet from Ukraine's President Zelenskyy. NBC News' Keir Simmons reports.

headlines.title: Justice Department urges judge not to block ‘anti-weaponization’ fund that it says is already dead
headlines.description: A federal judge last week temporarily blocked the $1.8 billion fund from being used after a Jan. 6 prosecutor sued to block the Trump administration’s actions.

headlines.title: Lesley Stahl, Bill Whitaker and Jon Wertheim say they’ll stay at ‘60 Minutes’
headlines.description: “60 Minutes” correspondents Leslie Stahl, Bill Whitaker and Jon Wertheim said Friday that they planned to stay on at the newsmagazine, capping days of turmoil.

headlines.title: Actor Anthony Head dies at age 72
headlines.description: British actor Anthony Head, best known for his roles in “Buffy the Vampire Slayer” and “Ted Lasso” has died at the age of 72 due to complications from pneumonia, his family said.

headlines.title: Astronauts back on board International Space Station after shelter in place order
headlines.description: Astronauts are back on board the International Space Station after NASA issued an order to shelter in place and prepare for evacuation as Russia attempted to fix a crack on its side of the station. NBC News' Ton Costello reports on the incident and what comes next in the repairs.

headlines.title: How the FBI and law enforcement plan to deploy drones to keep FIFA World Cup games safe
headlines.description: The FBI prepares to tackle drone security ahead of FIFA World Cup games spanning across the country. NBC News' Kelly O’Donnell goes behind the scenes with the FBI to see how its own drones and partnerships with local law enforcement will keep the events safe.

headlines.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
headlines.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

headlines.title: Democrat Xavier Becerra advances to general election in California governor’s race
headlines.description: Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra has advanced to the general election in the California governor’s race, NBC News projects

headlines.title: Janet Mills shows no sign of reigniting her Maine Senate bid amid Graham Platner’s troubles
headlines.description: Some of Maine Gov. Janet Mills’ most ardent supporters are encouraging her to reactivate her suspended Senate campaign as fellow Democrat Graham Platner faces new allegations of “toxic” behavior toward three ex-girlfriends, which he has denied

headlines.title: Son of James Handy’s girlfriend is charged with murder in actor’s stabbing death
headlines.description: A man has been charged with murder in the death of actor James Handy, who was found stabbed outside his girlfriend’s Los Angeles home this week, the district attorney’s office said Friday
